# Gustatory Receptor GR66 in Papilio xuthus Controls Host Plant Selection in Larvae

tags: #example #llm-draft

date: 2025-01-01
status: drafting
journal:
slug: example-project

---

## One-liner (imagine the opening sentence of the abstract at submission)

We show that knockout of the bitter taste receptor GR66 in Papilio xuthus larvae increases oviposition and feeding on rue (Ruta graveolens), a plant normally avoided.

## Central Claim

GR66 detects bitter compounds (furanocoumarins) and is the primary driver of avoidance signals in host plant selection.

## Target Journal

- First choice: eLife
- Second choice: PLOS Biology

## Scope

- In scope: Behavioral phenotype of GR66 KO, electrophysiology data, expression confirmation by RNA-seq
- Out of scope: Functional analysis of receptors other than GR66
